notes: First passing case: single-tile TILE-layout bf16 no-gamma path. Verifies the core two-pass pipeline (square -> reduce SUM/REDUCE_ROW -> rsqrt+eps -> Mul Col-bcast) and the TILE I/O path. RM, partial-W/H, gamma, fp32 paths not yet exercised.

cb_state: BRISC(reader) stuck at cb_reserve_back in read_sticks_for_tilize on cb_input_raw_rm (CB 0). TRISC(compute) stuck in tilize<Wt=2,cb_gamma_rm=2,cb_gamma_tiled=3> at wait_front on cb_gamma_rm. NCRISC(writer) stuck at cb_wait_front on cb_output_rm.

summary: Reader/Compute deadlock from non-interleaved gamma+input streaming on RM+gamma path.
detail: Reader does ALL input reads (Step 2: read_sticks_for_tilize aggregates all chunks) BEFORE starting gamma reads (Step 3: per-chunk loop). For RM+gamma, compute waits gamma on chunk 0 before input — but reader hasnt pushed gamma yet, and cant push gamma because its blocked on cb_input_raw_rm full (compute hasnt consumed input because its waiting for gamma). Fix: collapse reader to a per-chunk loop that pushes gamma BEFORE input each chunk, so the producer order matches the consumer order. This also fixes the (less symmetric) interleaving for TILE input + gamma path. The design CB Sync Summary still holds (per-chunk balance unchanged) — bug is purely temporal ordering in reader.

note: No tests ran — pytest -k expression contained = which is parsed as assignment. 0 collected/0 passed. Need to re-run with proper -k syntax (e.g. quote keys with hyphens). Exit code 0 triggered PASS hook but no fix verification occurred yet.
Test ID format mismatch — 0 tests collected, exit 0 triggered PASS hook spuriously. The actual parametrize ID order is gamma_mode=gamma not just gamma. Will retry with correct ID.

notes: Confirms the gamma path (stage E: x_norm * gamma with BroadcastDim::Row) and per-chunk gamma+input interleaving fixes the deadlock. Stage E + tilize-gamma asymmetric (total_input_pages=1) both functional.

note: ~150 tests; likely global script timeout. No triage.json generated. Will run smaller slices to bisect.

summary: Exit 143 (SIGTERM) on --run-all is most likely run_safe_pytest.sh per-run timeout, not a test failure. 150 tests * ~1.5s = ~4min minimum; first-time JIT compiles can push that significantly past the safe-pytest timeout. Best to run smaller slices (bf16/no_gamma first, then gamma, then fp32) and bisect to find any real failures or hangs.

notes: Validates RM path with partial-W (W=50, W=47), partial-H (H=17), wide-W (W=1024), and multibatch (NC=2). All without gamma.
